metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_352
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Exposure of mossy fibre neurons to Wnt7-containing culture medium derived from cerebellar granule cells increases the density of VAMP2, synapsin 1 and Bassoon puncta along axons. This is supported by an increase in the number of recycling synaptic vesicle sites as identified using antibodies against synaptotagmin-1 or FM1-43 labelling.

Conversely, VAMP2 puncta and number of recycling synaptic vesicle sites were significantly decreased in Dvl1 mutant mice or when Wnt7b signalling was interrupted by treatment with the Wnt inhibitor Sfrp-1. Dvl1 mutant mice further showed a decrease in the amount of synapsin 1 accumulating at cerebellar glomerular rosettes.

27/3/2017 Pim
- Since Dvl1 is a presumed scaffold of developing synapses I agree to (1) not select the "regulates ..." option and to (2) not advise a second annotation against "Wnt sinaling pathway in the presynapse" (Dvl1 does not signal).. Experimental description: Materials and Methods "Basilar pontine nuclei from wild-type, Dvl1 mutant, or Wnt7a-/-;Dvl1-/- mutant mice were dissected from newborn or P1 pups, cut into 100-μm2 explants, and cultured in 16-well LabTeck chamber slides, as previously described".

checks.label: OWL Consistency
checks.error_message: No inferences attributed to this model graph in the materializer output. Likely a parse failure or other materializer skip — re-run on this model alone to investigate.
